There are many reasons you may want to think long and hard about whether to kick your husband out or not.
You shouldn't do it unless you know for sure that he is not worth taking back.
Kicking your husband out can create issues with your children, especially if they're young.
Depending on the circumstances, your children may be more comforted to know that their father is still around, just not in their house.
In cases where you kick out your husband and both agree to separate, your children may grow up thinking of themselves as "damaged goods" because your marriage didn't work out.
If the reason you want to kick your husband out is that you think it has something to do with how well he treats you or is with you, you may want to look for another solution.
First, assess the situation.
There is also a chance that you haven't been nice to your husband, either.
Check your anger management. Ask your family, friends, and children if you're "always yelling" at your husband. 
Maybe you call your husband names, or look down upon him for his lack of Finnish in bachelors school, or tell him to go back to his father's home and realize that your marriage is not perfect.
Next, listen to your husband.
He may have been previously treated "very unfairly" and finds that it was "easier" to get involved with another woman.
Him going through an extra-marital affair and not telling you about it doesn't mean that you were perfect, either.
Ask him to be forthright as to why he's cheated on you.
Has he been successful with his career? 
Is he content with his house?
Does he have a good relationship with his children, and with you? 
It's time, if you can spare the money, to look for counseling.
Everyone needs counseling. You and your husband, if your family has money, should be scheduled for individual counseling, then try group counseling together.
Marital counseling needs to happen. You can also try couples counseling, together, or one on one to try and let him know how deeply you care about him.
Although it may feel like you are too far apart to ever be close, marital counseling brings families together by bringing couples together to search for common ground and ways to improve their marriage and lives.

question: I've had enough of my husband. What should I do?
answer: Angelina Only after girl left her family's home, husband decides to think family first. Resident Angelina shared a post online about how her husband who has 3 years' experience of fatherhood wants her to go out to work if she does not do the household chores herself. The rather unusual view by her husband has got her "culture-shocked" since her professorial husband is not lining her up to work but actually wants her to leave because she is not willing to shoulder duties as a wife and mother. "What am I supposed to do? This is the second time my husband wants me to leave home! The first time he told me is when I was in hospital!" /TwitterNET Her young daughter, whose voice supports her mom, has sure given us a good example of daughter should love her mother. There may be many aspects making it commonly accepted what should a woman do if not to leave home and work; There should be many aspects of your mind that we may ignore either consciously or unconsciously, for lack of experience and space to think more deeply. Angelina's daughter has got her "balance" and girl auto-mechanic has reminded us of the reason why having more skills is good. Sometimes, most of us are diversified and we "think differently". The lesson the young mother wants to teach us is that it has lot to do with "love supply". The second thing, we need to know "should a woman leave home or not? ". It is not straightforward. It is a advisory question which should be based on the deep-thinking of plural solutions. Take Angelina for example. If her daughter wakes up every morning, go to school, eat meals made by her uncle and take a nice rest, comes out to school to hobnob with friends then one may think this would be better as the mother is from another country and might not have basic mothering skills. However, one who drives a car goes back and forth to work or other matters, has also boosted the own confidence in the mom as they are doing things together. All of us need to find durable solutions to raise our own children; to raise children with a father is for sure a good idea, yet leaving them at home might be just fine. We should have full confidence of the reliability and ability of people who come in contact with our children. Angelina is leaving home at a crucial age of her daughter, for the sake of her career. While, what if she figured it out, leaving home for education today, and after graduating working to increase her income as a carrier? It is possible to have financial sources within certainty that can strengthen the family bond in many different ways.
